root.docs_md:
Background daemon that hosts persistent terminal processes across app restarts, plus
the processes-monitor webview page.

items.name: write_message_blocking
items.docs_md: Serialize a message to rkyv bytes, write as a length-prefixed frame (blocking).

items.name: write_message
items.docs_md:
Serialize a message to rkyv bytes, write as a length-prefixed frame.
Use: `write_message(&mut writer, &my_msg).await?`

items.name: read_message
items.docs_md:
Read a length-prefixed frame, deserialize from rkyv bytes.
Use: `let msg: Option<MyMsg> = read_message!(reader)?;`

items.name: read_message_blocking
items.docs_md:
Read a length-prefixed frame (blocking), deserialize from rkyv bytes.
Use: `let msg: Option<MyMsg> = read_message_blocking!(reader, MyMsg)?;`

items.name: PROCESSES_LIST_EVENT
items.docs_md: Event name for process list updates (host -> webview).

items.name: PROCESSES_NAVIGATE_EVENT
items.docs_md: Event name for process navigation (webview -> host).

items.name: ProcessesListEvent
items.docs_md: Service connection status + process list, sent periodically to the webview.

members.name: attached
members.docs_md: Whether a GUI terminal is attached to this process.

members.name: preview_lines
members.docs_md: Last few lines of terminal output for preview.

items.name: PreviewLine
items.docs_md: A simplified terminal line for the preview.

items.name: ProcessNavigateEvent
items.docs_md: Emitted by the processes webview when user clicks a process card.

members.name: navigate
members.docs_md: Discriminator so serde doesn't confuse with ProcessKillEvent.

items.name: ProcessKillEvent
items.docs_md: Emitted to kill a single service-managed process.

members.name: kill
members.docs_md: Discriminator so serde doesn't confuse with ProcessNavigateEvent.

items.name: ProcessKillAllEvent
items.docs_md: Emitted to kill all service-managed processes.

members.name: kill_all
members.docs_md: Discriminator field so serde doesn't match arbitrary IPC payloads.

items.name: CommandOrigin
items.docs_md:
Who issued a command relayed through the agent request plumbing. Most
`AgentCommandRequest`s originate from an agent (`Agent`), but some user
actions (e.g. opening a history entry) reuse the same path. A CLI agent is
identified by its MCP `anchor` (`ProcessId`); a page agent by its `sid`.

items.name: bundle_root_for
items.docs_md: Returns the `.app` root if `exe` lives inside a macOS bundle.

items.name: current_bundle_root
items.docs_md: Resolve bundle root for the currently-running executable.

items.name: is_bundled
items.docs_md: True if the running process lives inside a `.app` bundle.

items.name: EMBEDDED_AGENT_PLIST
items.docs_md: Plist filename of the embedded launchd agent (matches packaging/macos/ai.vmux.service.plist).

items.name: EMBEDDED_AGENT_LABEL
items.docs_md:
launchd label of the embedded agent (matches the <Label> in
packaging/macos/ai.vmux.service.plist).

items.name: ServiceConnection
items.docs_md:
Async client connection to the vmux service.
Wraps the Unix socket with framing/serialization.

items.name: ServiceHandle
items.docs_md:
Non-async handle for Bevy systems to communicate with the service.
Uses a background tokio task and std mpsc channels.

items.name: write_raw_frame
items.signature: pub fn write_raw_frame(writer: &mut W, data: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<()>
items.docs_md: Write a length-prefixed frame to an async writer.

items.name: read_raw_frame
items.docs_md:
Read a length-prefixed frame from an async reader.
Returns `None` on clean EOF.

items.name: write_raw_frame_blocking
items.signature: pub fn write_raw_frame_blocking(writer: &mut W, data: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<()>
items.docs_md: Write a length-prefixed frame to a blocking writer.

items.name: read_raw_frame_blocking
items.docs_md:
Read a length-prefixed frame from a blocking reader.
Returns `None` on clean EOF.

items.name: drive_sse
items.signature: pub fn drive_sse(request: reqwest::Request, parse_sse: crate::stream::ParseSse, tx: crossbeam_channel::Sender<crate::stream::StreamEvent>)

items.name: generate_plist
items.signature: pub fn generate_plist(profile: &str, binary_path: &std::path::Path, log_path: &std::path::Path) -> String
items.docs_md: Render the LaunchAgent plist XML for a profile.

items.name: install
items.docs_md: Write the plist for `profile` pointing at `binary_path`.

items.name: uninstall
items.docs_md: Remove the plist and unload from launchd.

items.name: ensure_running
items.docs_md:
Make sure the daemon is installed and running. Idempotent.
`binary_path` is the daemon executable (resolved by the caller).

items.name: ServicePlugin
items.docs_md:
Registers the services/processes-monitor webview page; the persistent-process server
itself runs in the `vmux_service` binary.

items.name: Process
items.docs_md: A single terminal process managed by the service.

items.name: PlacementMode
items.docs_md: How a spawned page is placed relative to its anchor pane.

members.name: Auto
members.docs_md:
Default: don't spawn a new pane unless necessary. Reuse the agent's
existing terminal region (stack the new terminal into it); split one pane
off the agent only when no region exists yet.

members.name: Split
members.docs_md: New pane split off the anchor pane (X/Y), in the given direction.

members.name: Stack
members.docs_md: New stack added to the anchor pane itself (Z); the pane keeps its size.

items.name: RECORD_STOP_TIMEOUT
items.docs_md:
Stop-recording round-trip bound. `finishWriting` after live encoding is
fast, but a large clip's moov flush can take a few seconds. Comfortably
under vibe's 60s MCP tool timeout.

members.name: ReadTerminalFull
members.docs_md:
Like `ReadTerminal` but returns the full scrollback history plus the
visible screen as plain text (used to capture a command's complete
output, not just the current viewport).

items.name: ClientMessage
items.docs_md: Messages sent from the GUI client to the service.

items.name: CopyModeKey
items.docs_md:
Vim-style visual/copy-mode action sent by the GUI to the service.

All movement keys (Left/Right/Up/Down/LineStart/LineEnd/PageUp/PageDown)
reposition the copy-mode cursor. If visual selection is active, movement
also extends the selection to the new cursor position.

members.name: Left
members.docs_md: Move cursor one cell left (clamped to col 0).

members.name: Right
members.docs_md: Move cursor one cell right (clamped to last column).

members.name: Up
members.docs_md: Move cursor one row up (clamped to row 0).

members.name: Down
members.docs_md: Move cursor one row down (clamped to last row).

members.name: LineStart
members.docs_md: Jump cursor to column 0 of the current row.

members.name: LineEnd
members.docs_md: Jump cursor to the last column of the current row.

members.name: LastNonBlank
members.docs_md: Jump cursor to the last non-blank cell of the current row (`g_`).

members.name: FirstNonBlank
members.docs_md: Jump cursor to the first non-blank cell of the current row (`^`).

members.name: WordForward
members.docs_md: Move to the next vi word start (`w`).

members.name: BigWordForward
members.docs_md: Move to the next whitespace-delimited WORD start (`W`).

members.name: WordBackward
members.docs_md: Move to the previous vi word start (`b`).

members.name: BigWordBackward
members.docs_md: Move to the previous whitespace-delimited WORD start (`B`).

members.name: WordEndForward
members.docs_md: Move to the next vi word end (`e`).

members.name: BigWordEndForward
members.docs_md: Move to the next whitespace-delimited WORD end (`E`).

members.name: WordEndBackward
members.docs_md: Move to the previous vi word end (`ge`).

members.name: BigWordEndBackward
members.docs_md: Move to the previous whitespace-delimited WORD end (`gE`).

members.name: Top
members.docs_md: Move to the first visible row (`gg`).

members.name: Bottom
members.docs_md: Move to the last visible row (`G`).

members.name: ScreenTop
members.docs_md: Move to the top visible row (`H`).

members.name: ScreenMiddle
members.docs_md: Move to the middle visible row (`M`).

members.name: ScreenBottom
members.docs_md: Move to the bottom visible row (`L`).

members.name: PrevParagraph
members.docs_md: Move to the previous paragraph/blank-line boundary (`{`).

members.name: NextParagraph
members.docs_md: Move to the next paragraph/blank-line boundary (`}`).

members.name: FindForward
members.docs_md: Find a character forward on the current line (`f{char}`).

members.name: FindBackward
members.docs_md: Find a character backward on the current line (`F{char}`).

members.name: TillForward
members.docs_md: Move until before a character forward on the current line (`t{char}`).

members.name: TillBackward
members.docs_md: Move until after a character backward on the current line (`T{char}`).

members.name: RepeatFindReverse
members.docs_md: Repeat the last find/till motion in reverse (`,`).

members.name: PageUp
members.docs_md: Move cursor up by half a screen.

members.name: PageDown
members.docs_md: Move cursor down by half a screen.

members.name: StartSelection
members.docs_md:
Re-anchor the selection at the current cursor position. Subsequent
movement keys extend the selection from this anchor.

members.name: StartLineSelection
members.docs_md:
Select full lines from the current cursor row. Subsequent movement
extends the linewise selection by row.

members.name: Copy
members.docs_md: Return the current selection text and exit copy mode.

members.name: Exit
members.docs_md: Discard any selection and exit copy mode.

items.name: CommandLifecycleKind
items.docs_md: Messages sent from the service to the GUI client.

items.name: ProcessInfo
items.docs_md: Metadata about a process, returned in ProcessList.

items.name: build_request
items.signature: pub fn build_request(model: &str, messages: &[crate::message::Message], tools: &[crate::stream::ToolDef], api_key: &str) -> reqwest::Request

items.name: build_request
items.signature: pub fn build_request(model: &str, messages: &[crate::message::Message], tools: &[crate::stream::ToolDef], api_key: &str) -> reqwest::Request

items.name: build_request
items.signature: pub fn build_request(model: &str, messages: &[crate::message::Message], tools: &[crate::stream::ToolDef], api_key: &str) -> reqwest::Request

items.name: ensure_running
items.signature: pub fn ensure_running(profile: &str, exe: &std::path::Path) -> Result<(), RegistrationError>

items.name: run_server
items.docs_md: Run the IPC server loop, accepting connections and dispatching messages.

items.name: run
items.docs_md:
Daemon entry point. Initializes logging, writes pid/identity, binds the socket,
installs SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, and runs the IPC server until shutdown.

submodules.docs_md:
Replace a running daemon: graceful Shutdown over the socket,
escalate to SIGTERM, finally SIGKILL.

items.name: wait_for_pid_exit
items.signature: pub fn wait_for_pid_exit(pid: i32, deadline: std::time::Instant) -> bool

items.name: send_signal
items.signature: pub fn send_signal(pid: i32, sig: i32) -> std::io::Result<()>

items.name: replace_running
items.signature: pub fn replace_running(pid: i32, send_shutdown: F) -> ReplaceOutcome
items.docs_md:
Replace the running daemon for the given pid.

`send_shutdown` performs the IPC Shutdown round-trip.
`Ok` means acknowledged or connection closed; `Err` means unreachable
(falls through to SIGTERM).

items.name: clean_runtime_files
items.docs_md: Best-effort cleanup of stale runtime files for the current profile.
